hero.sub: From a single coordinator to teams running programs across multiple sites, Vome automates the manual work behind onboarding, scheduling, tracking and communication. All in one platform, with unlimited volunteers on every plan.

feature_tabs.intro_sub: Vome is the complete volunteer management platform. One easy-to-use system that eliminates manual work at every step, from the first application to the final report.
feature_tabs.rec_headline: Build the recruitment process you need.
feature_tabs.rec_f1_body: Add questions, request file uploads, collect e-signatures, embed shift calendars — all from one form builder.
feature_tabs.rec_f2_body: Share direct links to opportunities, forms, or your public page. Embed on your site, post on social, or share it anywhere.
feature_tabs.rec_f3_body: Automations move applicants forward on their own and keep your system organized when knowing it's your turn to perform an admin task. Triggered by form response automations, visibility rules, and eligibility requirements.
feature_tabs.rec_f4_body: Email and app notifications keep volunteers in the loop through every step of the application process, by eliminating redundant back-and-forth, and without any administrative intervention.
feature_tabs.onb_f1_title: Build any onboarding flow, your way
feature_tabs.onb_f1_body: Combine forms, fillable PDFs, e-signatures, training modules, checklists, and file uploads into a single guided sequence. Organize into folders by program, department, or season.
feature_tabs.onb_f2_body: Waivers, releases, policies, and compliance forms. Signed and submitted inside the sequence, with no third-party tools, printing, scanning, or re-uploading needed.
feature_tabs.onb_f3_body: Due dates, reminders, skip rules, auto-expiration, and visibility settings per step. Choose whether volunteers propel themselves to the next step or admins advance them.
feature_tabs.onb_f4_body: Background check integrations, auto-expiring renewals for certifications and waivers, and audit-ready tracking at every step.
feature_tabs.sch_panel_sub: One-off events. Recurring tasks. Multi-site calendars. Vome handles it all.
feature_tabs.sch_f1_body: Create single shifts, recurring schedules, or import entire calendars from a spreadsheet. Use shift templates and apply them across programs for quick and easy setup. Mass assign, edit, and cancel from one calendar view.
feature_tabs.sch_f2_body: Choose instant booking or admin approval. Open shifts to everyone, or make them private and visible only to tagged volunteers. Let volunteers propose their own times with flexible scheduling.
feature_tabs.sch_f3_title: Group reservations for corporate and team events
feature_tabs.sch_f3_body: Volunteers reserve multiple spots in a single action on behalf of a team, a family, or a corporate group. Hours and participation roll up at the group level, ready for reporting.
feature_tabs.sch_f4_body: Configurable shift notifications send reminders before, during, and after each shift. Volunteers confirm, join waitlists, and sync shifts to Google, Outlook, or Apple Calendar.
feature_tabs.hrs_headline: So many ways to track volunteer hours. Pick what fits your program.
feature_tabs.hrs_f1_body: QR scan, tap-to-check-in via the app, on-site kiosk, or fully automate check-in/out. Pick one, or mix different methods across your different volunteer programs.
feature_tabs.hrs_f2_body: Require volunteers to be physically within a defined radius to check in or out. Customizable by program. Prevents remote check-ins and protects program data.
feature_tabs.hrs_f3_title: Walk-ins, group reservations, and off-site hours
feature_tabs.hrs_f3_body: On-site kiosks handle check-in and check-out for everyone on a shift, with an optional "Join a shift" mode for walk-ins who want to sign up on the spot. Group reservations roll up at the group level for reporting. Volunteers can also submit hour claims for work they did off-platform.
feature_tabs.hrs_f4_body: Shifts and hours by volunteer, a complete reservations and hours log, and custom database exports. Every hour traced to a person, an opportunity, and a shift.
feature_tabs.data_headline: Import your data. Organize it your way. Report in just a few clicks.
feature_tabs.data_f1_body: Move your volunteer records into Vome in minutes. Bulk import from any other system, map custom fields to match your existing structure, and invite volunteers to claim their profiles. Duplicate handling built in.
feature_tabs.data_f2_title: A database that flexes to your program
feature_tabs.data_f2_body: Segment volunteers by tag, program, or location with unlimited custom fields. Build and save custom views that filter and organize data any way you need. Give each admin access only to the data that belongs to them. Super admins see everything. Site managers see their site.
feature_tabs.data_f3_body: Hours by volunteer, attendance logs, sequence completion, form submissions, and check-in history. Every report you need for funders, boards, and audits, ready in seconds.
feature_tabs.data_f4_body: Visualize hours, shifts, onboarding progress, form completion, and custom field trends. Feel the pulse of your program without running a single report.
feature_tabs.com_headline: Every way you need to communicate, in one place.
feature_tabs.com_f1_body: One-on-one instant messaging, custom group chats, and auto-generated chatrooms for every shift and opportunity. No messaging apps. No email chains. Volunteers talk to admins and each other right inside the platform.
feature_tabs.com_f2_body: Send personalized emails to volunteers through Vome's server, or connect your own Google or Outlook account to send from your domain. Build reusable email templates with dynamic variables. Target by group, tag, program, or any custom field.
feature_tabs.com_f3_title: Automate the messages you send every week
feature_tabs.com_f3_body: Birthday wishes, shift reminders, onboarding follow-ups, survey invitations, award notifications, and confirmation requests. Set the rule once. Vome handles the sending forever.
feature_tabs.com_f4_title: Full visibility across your team's outgoing messages
feature_tabs.com_f4_body: Every email and notification in one dashboard. See what was sent, to whom, when, and by which admin. Share email templates across the team so everyone's communication stays consistent.

module_recruitment.page_title: Volunteer application forms & recruitment workflows | Vome
module_recruitment.page_description: Build modern, custom-branded forms with conditional questions, automations, and recruitment workflows that route applicants automatically.
module_recruitment.hero_sub: Modern forms with conditional questions, file uploads, and e-signatures. Automations that route applicants. Recruitment workflows wired into the rest of the platform.
module_recruitment.benefits_h2: Everything you need to convert interest into action
module_recruitment.benefits_sub: Redirect users from your website, social media, or email campaigns to custom forms that guide applicants through your recruitment process and save your team hours of manual work.
module_recruitment.ben1_title: A branded page for every opportunity
module_recruitment.ben1_desc: Build custom landing pages for events, programs, and positions. Each routes to its own form and recruitment workflow.
module_recruitment.ben2_desc: Show only what's relevant. Branch on each answer to personalize the volunteer's experience.
module_recruitment.ben3_desc: Route applicants by role, age, group status, or whether they're already in your database. Each volunteer sees only the form that fits them, and you decide what's visible to whom.
module_recruitment.ben4_desc: New applicants can be dropped straight into onboarding sequences automatically, with no manual triage, status updates, or assignments needed.
module_recruitment.ben5_desc: Filter, sort, and save custom views. Permissions let each team see only the forms that matter to them, across sites and locations.
module_recruitment.ben6_desc: Volunteers fill out forms on the iOS and Android apps, browser, tablet, or computer. Mobile-first by default, accessible everywhere.
module_recruitment.spot1_title: Build any application form, exactly the way you need it.
module_recruitment.spot1_body: Drag, drop, save. Add conditional questions that show or hide based on previous answers, request file uploads, collect digital consent through e-signatures, and embed shift calendars right inside the form.
module_recruitment.spot1_b1: 20+ question types to collect any data your form needs
module_recruitment.spot1_b2: Choose who gets notified about new submissions, and when
module_recruitment.spot1_b3: Link forms to different areas of the platform to collect submissions at every stage
module_recruitment.spot1_b4: Duplicate any form to spin up new ones with the same questions and workflow
module_recruitment.spot2_title: You decide what the volunteer journey looks like.
module_recruitment.spot2_body: Whether you use a single form for all volunteers, or different forms for every role, age group, or situation, it's all configurable in Vome. Set who can see each role, what they need to complete, and how they advance through the process.
module_recruitment.spot2_b1: Make opportunities visible only to matching volunteers by tag, role, location, age, or anything else you need
module_recruitment.spot2_b2: Add conditional questions to collect specific data based on previous answers
module_recruitment.spot2_b3: Track every submission on a live dashboard, monitor status, and take bulk actions in a few clicks
module_recruitment.spot2_b4: Sort, filter, and save custom views, and use admin permissions to share forms across sites while letting each team see only what they need
module_recruitment.spot3_title: Automations pick up where the form leaves off.
module_recruitment.spot3_body: When a form is submitted, automations fire. Assign onboarding sequences or opportunities, add tags, and send a confirmation email. Each action can be triggered by what the volunteer answered.
module_recruitment.spot3_b1: 10+ automations ready to use out of the box
module_recruitment.spot3_b2: Move volunteers through the process without manual work
module_recruitment.spot3_b3: Map form submissions directly into your database, structured exactly how you need it
module_recruitment.spot3_b4: Let your IT team push form submissions to any third-party platform with our open API
module_recruitment.midcta_sub: Build your first form free, in minutes.
module_recruitment.how_sub: Build once. Set the rules. Let Vome do the rest, every time someone applies.
module_recruitment.step1_desc: Drag-and-drop fields. Add conditional logic, file uploads, e-signatures, and shift-calendar embeds.
module_recruitment.step2_desc: Configure eligibility, screening questions, and the stages applicants will move through.
module_recruitment.step3_desc: Direct links, QR codes, and embeds. A branded recruitment page with featured opportunities.
module_recruitment.step4_desc: Submissions trigger the right next steps. Tags, sequences, confirmations, and assignments run on their own based on what each volunteer answered.
module_recruitment.diff_sub: Generic form tools collect data into a spreadsheet. Vome turns each submission into an end-to-end recruitment workflow.
module_recruitment.diff1_desc: Forms are one step of the volunteer journey. Choose which form a volunteer submits and how it shapes the next step, whether that's onboarding, scheduling shifts, or whatever your program needs.
module_recruitment.diff2_desc: Most form tools stop at conditional fields. Vome layers on conditional automations that move volunteers to the right stage based on how you've designed the workflow. Handle file uploads, background checks, or anything else you need without extra effort.
module_recruitment.diff3_desc: Forms can feed directly into your database, or be prompted throughout the platform whenever you need them. Require volunteers to resubmit forms before reserving shifts, checking in, or anything else.
module_recruitment.faq_sub: Common questions about application forms and recruitment workflows.
module_recruitment.faq2_a: Each question can branch based on a previous answer. Vome will show, hide, or require fields dynamically. Useful for personalizing the form by program, role, or eligibility.
module_recruitment.faq3_q: What happens after a form is submitted?
module_recruitment.faq3_a: You can configure automations to handle the next step automatically, or manually screen submissions and take action as needed. Based on what the applicant answered, Vome can tag them, assign them to an onboarding sequence, post them to the right opportunity, and send a confirmation email, all without manual work from your team.
module_recruitment.faq4_a: Yes. You can collect all sorts of file uploads from volunteers. Have them upload a document, digitally sign a waiver, or fill out a PDF directly inside Vome.
module_recruitment.faq5_q: Can I reuse a form across opportunities?
module_recruitment.faq5_a: Yes. You can customize the recruitment process and reuse forms across your opportunities in just a few clicks. You can also duplicate opportunities or forms with ease.
module_recruitment.faq6_a: Yes. Forms are mobile-first, and shareable links open the Vome app on logged-in volunteers' phones for an app-native experience.

module_onboarding.page_description: Build onboarding sequences with interviews, training, waivers, e-signatures, and Sterling Volunteers background checks. Track status and expirations in one place.
module_onboarding.hero_sub: Combine file uploads, background checks, and training in guided flows. Decide what volunteers do, when steps expire, and how completion is verified, all with ease.
module_onboarding.benefits_sub: Reduce the time it takes to move volunteers through the onboarding process. Decide which steps volunteers can see and when they can move through them, designed exactly as you need it, with a live dashboard to track progress.
module_onboarding.ben1_desc: Build as many onboarding processes as you need, whether by role or age specification. Volunteers can get auto-assigned the moment their application clears.
module_onboarding.ben2_desc: Connect the tools you already use, like Sterling Volunteers or Verified First, without leaving Vome. Statuses sync back automatically.
module_onboarding.ben3_desc: Drop SCORM-compliant training modules directly into a sequence with our SCORM Cloud integration. Completion and scores sync to the volunteer profile. Upload videos or YouTube links and track who's watched them, often useful for training.
module_onboarding.ben4_desc: Collect signed waivers, let volunteers fill out PDFs directly in Vome, and gather form submissions inside the flow. Configure expirations and pull reports out of the box.
module_onboarding.ben5_desc: Volunteers see their next step from the Vome mobile app or web. Or keep any step (or the full sequence) private and advance volunteers yourself.
module_onboarding.ben6_desc: A dashboard view of who's screened, who's expiring, and who's waiting. Permissions scope each admin to their site or team.
module_onboarding.spot1_title: Build custom step-by-step onboarding sequences and choose who gets routed into them and when.
module_onboarding.spot1_body: Combine interviews, orientations, training modules, waivers, document uploads, and background checks into guided flows. Mark steps as required or optional, private or visible to volunteers, and so much more.
module_onboarding.spot1_b1: 10+ step types to choose from when building each step in your sequence
module_onboarding.spot1_b2: Customizable settings to control what volunteers see, whether they can skip steps, and when each step is due
module_onboarding.spot1_b3: Add automations that fire when volunteers finish a sequence or complete a specific step
module_onboarding.spot1_b4: Duplicate sequences to spin up new onboarding processes with similar configurations
module_onboarding.spot2_title: See exactly where every volunteer stands across every step.
module_onboarding.spot2_body: Volunteers can move through steps from their homepage, or admins can manage the process for them. A live dashboard shows where each volunteer is in their journey and how long they've been on a step. Build reports in minutes.
module_onboarding.spot2_b1: Volunteers see their sequences on the web and mobile apps
module_onboarding.spot2_b2: Uploaded files and documents stay on each volunteer's profile for easy access
module_onboarding.spot2_b3: Manage file expirations with ease and let volunteers reupload new versions
module_onboarding.spot2_b4: Integrate third-party tools or pull sequence data using Vome's API
module_onboarding.spot3_title: Choose what comes after they finish onboarding.
module_onboarding.spot3_body: When volunteers complete the process and become eligible, Vome can automatically assign them to opportunities, let them reserve shifts, and turn them into active volunteers in your portal. Or assign them to a new sequence based on specific requirements they still need to complete. The possibilities are endless. Structure the program exactly as you need.
module_onboarding.spot3_b1: Set up automations per step, or trigger them once a volunteer finishes the full sequence
module_onboarding.spot3_b2: Send custom notifications to admins and volunteers when steps are completed or pending review
module_onboarding.spot3_b3: Run background checks or e-learning modules without leaving the platform
module_onboarding.spot3_b4: Auto-trigger expiration emails so volunteers can resubmit documents once they're past due
module_onboarding.midcta_sub: Build your first onboarding sequence free, in minutes.
module_onboarding.how_sub: Build the sequence once. Every volunteer who applies gets walked through the right screening for their role.
module_onboarding.step1_desc: Add interview, training, waiver, background-check, and document steps in the order your program needs.
module_onboarding.step2_desc: Sterling Volunteers and SCORM Cloud are native. Activate them once, reuse across every opportunity.
module_onboarding.step3_desc: Each volunteer sees their next step and takes action, or admins keep it private and advance them.
module_onboarding.step4_desc: Completion, expiration, and renewal sync back automatically. Reports and dashboards reflect it in real time.
module_onboarding.diff_sub: Generic onboarding tools track tasks. Vome lets you build custom onboarding journeys, immediately accessible after a volunteer completes an application form, all tied to your database.
module_onboarding.diff1_desc: Integrate background checks, Vome forms, and training videos directly throughout your sequence. Everything is tracked and managed inside Vome, and accessible on each volunteer's profile at any time.
module_onboarding.diff2_desc: See exactly when documents were uploaded and when steps were marked complete, across all sequences or per sequence. Pull the report you need in a few clicks.
module_onboarding.diff3_desc: Rochester Regional Health used Vome sequences to take volunteer onboarding from 6–8 weeks down to 1, saving admins 10+ hours every week. See why you can do the same and get started today.
module_onboarding.faq_sub: Common questions about onboarding sequences, screening, and expiration tracking.
module_onboarding.faq1_q: What step types can I add to a sequence?
module_onboarding.faq1_a: You can build any custom step. For example: interviews, orientations, trainings, waivers, e-signatures, fillable PDFs, file uploads, videos, checklists, background checks, and Vome forms.
module_onboarding.faq3_q: Can I let volunteers track their own progress?
module_onboarding.faq3_a: Yes. Each sequence can be set to volunteer-visible (they see their next step and take action themselves) or admin-only (your team advances them). You can mix modes per sequence depending on the need.
module_onboarding.faq4_q: How are expirations and renewals handled?
module_onboarding.faq4_a: Steps that expire (waivers, background checks, certifications) carry a renewal date on the volunteer record. Vome flags upcoming expirations on the dashboard, sends reminders before the date, and re-opens the step so the volunteer can resubmit. Past expirations are surfaced in a dedicated report.
module_onboarding.faq5_a: Yes. Build a sequence once and assign it to any number of opportunities, or duplicate a sequence to spin up a variation. Multi-site permissions scope sequences to the right admins so teams only manage what's theirs.
module_onboarding.faq6_q: Where does the volunteer data go?
module_onboarding.faq6_a: The data is available in many places: on your sequence dashboard, the volunteer's profile, and across our standard reports. You can easily access completion dates, expirations, upload dates, and so much more without any additional configuration.

module_scheduling.spot1_b1: Save any shift setup as a template and reuse it for next season's program, no rebuilding from scratch
module_scheduling.spot1_b2: Bulk-edit existing shifts: change capacity, times, spots, or coordinators across hundreds of shifts in a single action

module_scheduling.spot1_b4: Require a form, training, or background check before a volunteer can book, with recruitment and onboarding wired into the schedule

module_scheduling.spot2_b1: Vome flags reservation conflicts before they happen, with no double-bookings and no overlaps
module_scheduling.spot2_b2: Mass-assign volunteers across many shifts in a single action, straight from the schedule view
module_scheduling.spot2_b3: Mobile check-in from the volunteer app on arrival, or scan a shared QR kiosk at the door
module_scheduling.spot2_b4: Waitlists auto-promote volunteers when a spot opens, with notifications fired automatically
module_scheduling.spot3_title: Coordinate every shift without chasing anyone.
module_scheduling.spot3_body: Configure custom notification policies that fire dynamic messages before, during, and after every shift, by email, push, or in-app. Each shift can also get its own auto-generated chatroom with attendees added on reservation, so coordinators can drop last-minute updates to the whole crew. Volunteers sync to Google, Outlook, or Apple Calendar, and Vome fires reminders automatically.
module_scheduling.spot3_b1: Mark absences and log hours directly from the shift view, with no separate tracker and no copy-paste
module_scheduling.spot3_b2: Assign a coordinator to each shift so volunteers always know who to contact, and admins know who's accountable
module_scheduling.spot3_b3: Custom notification policies: dynamic messages before, during, and after every shift, through the channel you choose
module_scheduling.spot3_b4: Export the daily roster to PDF, Excel, or CSV with attendees, contact info, group rosters, and any custom fields
module_scheduling.midcta_sub: Start a 30-day free trial and schedule your first shift in minutes.
module_scheduling.how_sub: Build the shifts. Set the policy. Volunteers reserve. Vome handles the rest.
module_scheduling.step1_desc: Bulk-create one-time or recurring shifts. Set capacity, dates, and which volunteers can see them.
module_scheduling.step2_desc: Choose instant booking, approval, or admin-assign. Configure cancellations, check-ins, and reservation limits.
module_scheduling.step3_desc: From the app, browser, or a public calendar embedded on a form. Group reservations covered.
module_scheduling.step4_desc: Reminders fire, the chatroom is live, attendees check in, and the schedule export is ready when you need it.

module_scheduling.diff2_desc: One volunteer books for a team, family, or corporate group, with guests included. Each shift carries its own chatroom and check-in window. Volunteers scan a QR or check in from the mobile app, and hours log automatically.
module_scheduling.diff3_desc: 1.25M+ shifts scheduled across hospital systems, national events, and multi-site programs. Vome handles spikes and routine alike, on web and mobile.

module_scheduling.faq1_q: Can I create recurring shifts in bulk?
module_scheduling.faq1_a: Yes. Pick a date range, the days of the week, and the time window. Vome generates every shift in that range with the capacity and visibility rules you configured. You can edit any single shift afterward without breaking the recurring pattern.
module_scheduling.faq2_a: Policies control capacity, cancellation windows, check-in rules, and how many reservations a volunteer can hold at a time. Set a default policy for the org and override per opportunity. Vome enforces the rules at booking time and at check-in, so volunteers can't book past the limits.
module_scheduling.faq3_q: Can volunteers book for a group?
module_scheduling.faq3_a: Yes. Group reservations let one volunteer book on behalf of a team, family, or corporate group. Vome stores the group name, the lead, and the party size on the shift. The advanced Groups module ties group reservations back to dedicated groups for cleaner workflows and analytics.
module_scheduling.faq4_a: Each shift can have an auto-generated chatroom. As volunteers reserve, they're added automatically. Coordinators message the whole group at once for last-minute updates. The chatroom is optional per shift, and access ends when the shift does.
module_scheduling.faq5_q: Can I save a shift setup as a template?
module_scheduling.faq5_a: Yes. Save any shift or recurring pattern as a template, including capacity, visibility rules, scheduling policy, and all. Reuse the template next season or for a similar program in one click, no rebuilding from scratch.
module_scheduling.faq6_q: What kind of reporting comes built in?
module_scheduling.faq6_a: Out-of-the-box reports for shift activity by site, location, opportunity, hours completed, and more. Save custom views and filters, attach shift tags, and scope each admin to what they manage with permissions. Export to PDF, Excel, or CSV anytime.

module_hours.page_title: Volunteer hour tracking & check-in software | Vome
module_hours.page_description: Track volunteer hours your way. QR-code check-in via the mobile app, on-site kiosks on any device, volunteer hour claims, mass logging, and integrated reservation and hours reports.
module_hours.hero_sub: Pick the tools that fit your program: QR-code check-in, on-site kiosks, hour claims, mass logging, and GPS proximity validation. Every hour flows straight into reservation and hours reports.
module_hours.benefits_h2: Hour tracking that fits every kind of shift.
module_hours.benefits_sub: Some volunteers scan a QR code, others tap in at a kiosk, a few submit hour claims after the fact. Pick the method per opportunity. Every hour flows into the same profile, the same reservation record, and the same set of reports.
module_hours.ben1_desc: Volunteers can scan a QR code from the app, tap to check in from any device, or trigger a hands-free check-in via GPS proximity. Vome auto-checks-out anyone who forgets at shift end, and hours flow straight to the profile with no manual entry.
module_hours.ben2_desc: Turn any tablet, phone, or computer into a self-service kiosk. Volunteers find their name, tap to check in, and the kiosk groups them by opportunity or category for fast lookup at busy events.
module_hours.ben3_desc: Volunteers can submit hours for any shift they completed, even ones that weren't booked through Vome. Approve, decline, or request more info in one click, and the time lands on their profile.
module_hours.ben4_desc: Log hours for an entire shift in a few clicks, or backfill past shifts in bulk with accurate dates preserved. No spreadsheet edits, no per-volunteer data entry.
module_hours.ben5_desc: Configure attendance rules per opportunity: pick the check-in methods, optional GPS proximity, grace windows, and claim policies that fit each program.
module_hours.ben6_desc: Built-in line graphs, monthly trends, and check-in heatmaps. Save custom views and filters per admin, group hours any way you need, and export to Excel or sync to any platform using Vome's API.
module_hours.spot1_body: Pick the check-in flow that matches each opportunity. Some programs need no check-in at all. Vome auto-logs hours based on the shift's start and end times. From there, choose how volunteers confirm: scan a QR code from the app, just tap to check in with no scan needed, require GPS proximity to the site, or run an on-site Attendance Kiosk where volunteers find their name and check themselves in and out.
module_hours.spot1_b1: Configurable check-in windows per opportunity: early grace, late grace, and lock-out times to match how each program runs
module_hours.spot1_b2: Volunteers see the shift name, time, and role on screen before they confirm, so there are no wrong-shift mistakes
module_hours.spot1_b3: Coordinators can manually adjust hours, mark absences, or log past-shift attendance from the same view
module_hours.spot1_b4: Hours flow straight to the volunteer profile and reservation record, ready for reports the moment the shift ends
module_hours.spot2_title: Approve hour claims without chasing emails.
module_hours.spot2_body: You can choose to allow volunteers to submit hour claims for any shift they completed, even ones not booked through Vome. Each claim shows up in a queue with the shift, the volunteer, and the hours. Approve it and the time lands on their profile, ready for reports.
module_hours.spot2_b1: Bulk approve or decline multiple claims at once
module_hours.spot2_b2: Require volunteers to add a note or submit a form attached to their hour claim
module_hours.spot2_b3: Per-opportunity claim policies: max hours per submission, mandatory descriptions, attached evidence
module_hours.spot2_b4: Choose to allow volunteers to request additional hours, even if they've already been logged
module_hours.spot3_title: Reservation and hours reports, in one view.
module_hours.spot3_body: Every check-in, claim, and bulk log feeds the same dashboard. Slice by site, program, opportunity, or custom field. Compare reservation counts to hours actually logged. Export to Excel or sync with any platform using Vome's API.
module_hours.spot3_b1: Built-in line graphs, monthly trends, and check-in heatmaps, so you can see exactly when volunteers actually show up
module_hours.spot3_b2: Mix and match report types: shift list, attendees & hours, volunteer bookings, check-in/out, all from one place
module_hours.spot3_b3: Save custom views and filters as permissioned dashboards for each admin or site
module_hours.spot3_b4: Auto-summary cards (total hours, top sites, average shifts/volunteer) calculated for you, no spreadsheet pivot tables required
module_hours.midcta_sub: Start a 30-day free trial and start tracking hours with ease.
module_hours.how_sub: Pick the check-in method per opportunity. Vome handles the rest, from arrival to report.
module_hours.step1_desc: QR code, on-site kiosk, hour claims, or mass logging. Each opportunity can use a different combination.
module_hours.step2_desc: Scan from the app, tap into a kiosk, or wait to submit a claim later. GPS proximity is optional.
module_hours.step3_desc: Approved time lands on the volunteer profile and the opportunity record without manual data entry.
module_hours.step4_desc: Filter by site, program, or custom field. Export to Excel or sync to Google Sheets for funders and grants.
module_hours.diff_sub: Generic time-trackers ask volunteers to track their hours themselves. Vome offers 5+ methods to track hours systematically or automatically, and has all the reporting you need out of the box.
module_hours.diff1_desc: QR scan, kiosk tap, hour claim, mass log. Whatever fits the program. Every minute lands in the same volunteer profile and report.
module_hours.diff2_desc: Hours are matched to the shift each volunteer was booked for, so you can compare reserved time to time actually served.
module_hours.diff3_desc: Group hours by site, program, or custom field. Export to Excel or sync with the tools you already use through Vome's API.
module_hours.faq_sub: Common questions about volunteer check-in, hour claims, and reporting.
module_hours.faq1_a: Customize and print one QR to be used across your program. Reserved attendees scan it from the Vome app on their phone to check in and out. The same QR code can be reused every shift, every day, with no need to print a new one. Hours log automatically on the volunteer's profile and reports.
module_hours.faq2_q: Do I need to buy hardware for the kiosk?
module_hours.faq2_a: No. The on-site kiosk is a web page that runs on any tablet, phone, or computer you already have. Open the kiosk URL, lock the device to that page, and you're set. You can run as many kiosks as you need, customized per location.
module_hours.faq3_q: What happens when a volunteer forgets to check out?
module_hours.faq3_a: You can configure an automatic check-out at the end of the shift, so volunteers who forget still get credited for their reserved time. Admins can also adjust hours manually from the attendee list at any point.
module_hours.faq4_q: Can volunteers log hours for shifts that weren't booked through Vome?
module_hours.faq4_a: Yes. Volunteers can submit hour claims for any shift they completed, even off-platform volunteering. The claim shows up in your review queue with the shift, date, and hours. Approve it and the time lands on their profile.
module_hours.faq5_q: Can admins log hours in bulk?
module_hours.faq5_a: Yes. Pull up the attendee list for a shift or filter past volunteers, then log hours for the whole group in a few clicks. Dates and times stay accurate without manual data entry per person.
module_hours.faq6_q: How do hours feed into reports?
module_hours.faq6_a: Every method (QR scan, kiosk, claim, bulk log) writes to the same volunteer profile and reservation record. Reports group hours by volunteer, opportunity, site, program, or custom field, and can be exported to Excel or any third-party platform.

module_recognition.page_title: Volunteer recognition: Vome Achievements & custom awards | Vome
module_recognition.page_description: Design custom awards your team owns end to end — Volunteer of the Month, five-year tenure, anything you want. Every volunteer also earns Vome Achievements, a free Bronze-to-Platinum tier ladder, on every plan.
module_recognition.hero_sub: Design custom awards to reward your volunteers for the amazing work they do — Volunteer of the Month, #1 leader in hours, reaching a milestone, or anything you want.
module_recognition.benefits_h2: Recognition that lands the same week the work happens
module_recognition.benefits_sub: Two systems that complement each other. Your team can design custom awards based on the milestones you set, and decide which volunteers are eligible to earn them. Vome also has an out-of-the-box Bronze-to-Platinum achievement ladder that runs in the background for every volunteer.
module_recognition.ben1_desc: Build your own awards with their own name, icon or uploaded image, and description. Pick an auto rule Vome enforces or a manual award a coordinator can assign as needed, with optional caps on the number of recipients and a choice of whether it repeats on a continuous basis.
module_recognition.ben2_desc: Vome Achievements ship on every plan: a four-tier badge ladder fueled by Vome Points that volunteers earn by completing platform challenges. Active from a volunteer's first log, no admin setup.
module_recognition.ben3_title: Triggers on hours or shifts, any window
module_recognition.ben3_desc: Set thresholds in hours or shifts across all-time, a calendar year, a date range, a rolling window, or consecutive periods (e.g. 5 hours per month for 3 straight months).
module_recognition.ben4_title: Send a one-off award by hand
module_recognition.ben4_desc: Some moments do not fit a rule. Send a custom award to one volunteer or a hand-picked group, and choose to make it visible to volunteers on their profiles.
module_recognition.ben5_desc: When an award is given, you can choose to set up automated emails or notifications to the volunteer at a time you choose, using your template, sender, and signature. Admins get notified too, so coordinators see recognition as it happens.
module_recognition.ben6_title: Eligibility scoped to the right people
module_recognition.ben6_desc: Restrict any award by tag, opportunity, site, finished onboarding sequence, active database status, or even age range. Volunteers outside the scope never see it in their catalog or progress.
module_recognition.spot1_title: Design custom awards for the moments only you see.
module_recognition.spot1_body: Every award gets a name and a face, a shape and a scope. Some awards are simple — a one-time thank-you for coming. Others are more intricate — a tenure award capped at five recipients a year, restricted to your hospital site, with progress hidden until earned. The depth is there when you need it.
module_recognition.spot1_b1: Preset icons or upload your own image
module_recognition.spot1_b2: Cap how many volunteers can earn it, mark it repeatable or one-time
module_recognition.spot1_b3: Folders & bulk actions to keep a large award catalog clean
module_recognition.spot1_b4: Decide if volunteers can see their progress toward an award, or keep that tracking for admins only
module_recognition.spot2_title: A four-tier ladder every volunteer climbs, on every plan.
module_recognition.spot2_body: Vome ships a platform-level recognition ladder out of the box: Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum. Volunteers earn Vome Points by completing challenges tied to real activity, then climb the tiers as VP add up. Active from the first log, free on every plan, managed by Vome.
module_recognition.spot2_b1: Four tiers, Bronze through Platinum, fueled by Vome Points
module_recognition.spot2_b2: Challenges tied to real activity: hours logged, shifts completed, milestones hit
module_recognition.spot2_b3: Volunteers track tier progress and Vome Points in real time
module_recognition.spot2_b4: Free for every volunteer in every org, no setup required
module_recognition.spot3_title: A timeline volunteers can keep, and you can verify.
module_recognition.spot3_body: Each tier badge and award lands with a date and source. Vome Achievements show what challenges were completed to earn badges. Custom awards show who sent them or which rule matched. You can customize all the options below.
module_recognition.spot3_b1: Chronological wall on the volunteer profile, mobile and web
module_recognition.spot3_b2: Email or push notification fires the moment an award lands
module_recognition.spot3_b3: Progress bars for the next tier and any in-flight custom awards
module_recognition.spot3_b4: Admins can get notified too, so coordinators see what awards have been given
module_recognition.midcta_sub: Start recognizing your volunteers with achievements and custom awards. Sign up free today.
module_recognition.diff_h2: Recognition wired into the rest of the platform
module_recognition.diff_sub: Standalone gamification tools sit in their own silo. Vome's volunteer recognition software pulls from our scheduling and hour-tracking tools that help you organize the rest of your operations. It takes no additional time to reward volunteers, as it should be.
module_recognition.diff1_title: Same data as your hour tracking
module_recognition.diff1_desc: Auto rules run on the verified hours and shifts your team already approves. No separate counter, no double-counting, no reconciliation step.
module_recognition.diff2_desc: An award's eligibility, scope, and history live on the volunteer record itself. Coordinators see who earned what alongside reservations, onboarding, and tags, not on a separate tab.
module_recognition.diff3_desc: Recognition isn't a separate dashboard, a leaderboard, or a points minigame. The Vome tier ladder runs in the background; your custom awards reflect verified work. Volunteers get a real thank-you, not synthetic ranks.
module_recognition.faq_sub: Common questions about achievements and custom awards.
module_recognition.faq1_q: What's the difference between Vome Achievements and a custom award?
module_recognition.faq1_a: Vome Achievements are a platform-wide four-tier badge ladder, Bronze through Platinum, fueled by Vome Points. Every volunteer earns them by completing platform challenges. They are free on every plan and managed by Vome. Custom awards are completely designed by your team, with your own branding and rules. You decide who can earn them and how they are earned.
module_recognition.faq2_q: When does a custom award actually trigger?
module_recognition.faq2_a: The moment a logged hour or shift crosses the threshold you set. You pick the metric (hours or shifts) and the window: all-time, calendar year, a fixed date range, a rolling window of N days/weeks/months, or consecutive periods (e.g. 5 hours per month for 3 straight months). Vome uses the same hours and shifts your team already approves, so awards reflect verified work.
module_recognition.faq3_q: Can I send an award to a single volunteer with a personal note?
module_recognition.faq3_a: Yes. Custom awards can be sent manually to one volunteer or a hand-picked group, with a note attached. Useful for moments that no rule could capture: a volunteer who covered a hard shift, a five-year tenure that sits outside your usual milestones, or a thank-you at the end of a project.
module_recognition.faq4_q: What happens when an award lands? Who gets notified?
module_recognition.faq4_a: You can choose to have Vome email the volunteer the moment an award fires, at a time you choose, using your template, sender (the creator or a specific admin), and signature. Email variables like first name, award title, and window period are personalized per volunteer. You can also add admins who get notified each time the award is earned, so coordinators see recognition as it happens. This can all run through Vome's email system or through your integrated email (Outlook or Gmail).
module_recognition.faq5_q: Can I limit an award to a specific group of volunteers?
module_recognition.faq5_a: Yes. Each custom award can be scoped by tag, opportunity, site, completion of a specific onboarding sequence, active database status, or even an age range with configurable behavior when age is unknown. Volunteers outside the scope never see the award in their catalog or progress, so the experience stays clean for everyone else.
module_recognition.faq6_a: Vome Achievements (the Bronze-to-Platinum tier ladder and Vome Points) ship free on every plan, including Recruit. Custom awards (your own awards with auto rules or manual sending, eligibility scoping, and notifications) unlock on Enterprise.

module_comms.page_title: Volunteer communications: bulk email, chat, automated reminders | Vome
module_comms.page_description: Bulk and individual email from your own Google or Microsoft mailbox, native private and group chats, auto-generated chatrooms per opportunity and shift, and automations that fire on triggers.
module_comms.hero_sub: Send bulk and individual emails from your own Google or Microsoft mailbox, communicate using 1-1, group or shift-based chatrooms, and create reusable notifications for shift reminders, birthdays, and volunteer recognition.
module_comms.benefits_h2: Every volunteer channel, in one place
module_comms.benefits_sub: One workspace for every conversation with your volunteers, tied to the same database and the same audit trail. Stop juggling personal phones, WhatsApp groups, and a separate email tool: bulk email, native chat, shared templates, and reusable notification policies all live where your team already works.
module_comms.ben1_desc: Send a one-off message to a single volunteer or a targeted blast to a saved segment. Filter by tag, role, site, or status, all from inside Vome.
module_comms.ben2_desc: Connect a Google or Microsoft mailbox and outgoing email lands from your domain instead of a generic noreply address. Replies come back to your inbox.
module_comms.ben3_desc: Message volunteers one-on-one from the web or using the Vome app on the go. Conversations stay tied to their profile and accessible by each of your admins depending on their permissions.
module_comms.ben4_desc: Create custom group chats for ongoing teams. Vome also allows you to auto-generate chatrooms per opportunity and per shift, with admins added automatically.
module_comms.ben5_desc: Build notification policies to automate communication about shift reminders and post-shift surveys, birthday wishes, or between admins. You decide the content, where and when it's sent, and Vome automates the rest.
module_comms.ben6_desc: Build a template library and folder it so every coordinator sends with the same voice. Reuse the same templates inside one-off emails, bulk sends, and notification policies.
module_comms.spot1_title: Email volunteers like a marketer, from your own mailbox.
module_comms.spot1_body: You can filter your volunteer database into segments by tag, role, site, or status, then send a personalized email to each one through your own Google or Microsoft mailbox. Replies come back to your inbox, the dashboard tracks deliveries, and your team sees what was sent and to whom.
module_comms.spot1_b1: Cc, Bcc, attachments, custom subject headers, and a default signature on every send
module_comms.spot1_b2: You can also skip the integration and send through Vome's native email server in two clicks
module_comms.spot1_b3: Personalize with dynamic variables like first name, role, site, shift, and custom fields
module_comms.spot1_b4: Shared outbox tracks every send and exposes it to the team per your sharing policy
module_comms.spot2_title: Private chats, group chats, and a chatroom for every shift.
module_comms.spot2_body: There's no need to run your program through various messaging platforms. Vome allows you to private chat with any volunteer, create custom group chats for ongoing teams, and choose to benefit from auto-generated chatrooms per opportunity and per shift. When a volunteer reserves the shift, they join. When they cancel, they leave. It's that simple.
module_comms.spot2_b1: Send images, video, voice notes, documents, and links inline (not just text)
module_comms.spot2_b2: Assign or share a shift right inside the conversation, no tab-switching
module_comms.spot2_b3: Mute a thread, pin a message, jump to the volunteer's profile in one tap
module_comms.spot2_b4: Push notifications on the iOS and Android apps so volunteers actually see it
module_comms.spot3_title: Design the notifications you want, when you want them to send, throughout the entire platform.
module_comms.spot3_body: Build custom shift notification policies for pre-shift reminders, during shifts, or a post-shift survey link, then attach them to the shifts they were designed for. Add automated birthday notifications with site-level recipient rules, and hand-off notifications that ping coordinators when a profile lands in their site, sequence, opportunity, or shift, manually or via automation.
module_comms.spot3_b1: Compose every message with shared templates, dynamic variables, and your default signature
module_comms.spot3_b2: Pick the channel per rule: integrated email domain, in-app, push notification, or all of the above
module_comms.spot3_b3: Drop a survey link in the post-shift email so feedback comes in while it's fresh
module_comms.spot3_b4: Separate outbox tabs for shift notifications and birthday emails make every send auditable
module_comms.midcta_h2: Connect your mailbox and turn on policies in minutes.
module_comms.midcta_sub: Link your Google or Microsoft mailbox, create your templates, and let automations handle the rest.
module_comms.diff_sub: Generic email tools blast a list. WhatsApp groups lose volunteers when phones change. Vome is built for volunteer programs and connects to the rest of your data.
module_comms.diff1_desc: You can build custom segments with live filters of the database, not exports. Tag a volunteer or change their status and the next bulk send picks it up automatically.
module_comms.diff2_title: Email & chat under one audit trail
module_comms.diff2_desc: Outbox, chat history, and automation logs all live in the same admin dashboard. Filter by sender, segment, or trigger; control visibility with the email sharing policy.
module_comms.diff3_title: Coordinators stop leaving with the data
module_comms.diff3_desc: When a coordinator goes on leave, you keep their conversations, templates, and policies. Onboard the replacement in an afternoon instead of rebuilding a year of context from screenshots and personal text threads.
module_comms.faq_sub: Common questions about email, chat, and message automations.
module_comms.faq1_q: Can volunteers see emails come from my address?
module_comms.faq1_a: Yes. Connect a Google or Microsoft mailbox and Vome sends through it. Outgoing email lands from your address, replies route back to your inbox, and your domain reputation drives deliverability.
module_comms.faq2_q: Can our team share email templates so we all sound the same?
module_comms.faq2_a: Yes. Build templates and group them into folders that every admin can pull from. The same library powers one-off emails, bulk sends, shift notification policies, and birthday notifications, so the language stays consistent across coordinators and across automations.
module_comms.faq3_a: Vome creates a chatroom for every opportunity and for every shift. Admins are added automatically. Volunteers join when they reserve the shift and leave when they cancel. You can also build custom group chats for ongoing teams, and inside any chat you can send images, documents, voice notes, or assign a shift directly.
module_comms.faq4_q: How do shift notification policies work?
module_comms.faq4_a: A shift notification policy is a saved set of automated messages that fire around the shifts it's attached to: pre-shift reminders, during-shift updates, and post-shift follow-ups (with optional survey links). Build it once, attach it to any shift or shift generator, and every shift that uses the policy inherits the same rules. The full setup walkthrough lives in our help center.
module_comms.faq5_q: Can I control what other admins see in the outbox?
module_comms.faq5_a: Yes. The shared outbox lists every email sent through Vome (one-off, bulk, shift notifications, birthday notifications) and the email sharing policy controls who sees what. Filter by sender, segment, or trigger to audit a campaign or onboard a new coordinator without losing context.
module_comms.faq6_a: When a profile is assigned to a site, sequence, opportunity, or shift you coordinate, hand-off notifications ping you so the volunteer doesn't fall through the cracks. They fire whether the assignment was manual (another admin moved them) or automatic (a form submission or finished sequence routed them to you), and you can subscribe per category in your notification settings.

module_data.page_description: A custom volunteer database with mass import, unlimited custom fields, profile tags, saved custom views, Google Sheets sync, instant reports, and analytics dashboards.
module_data.hero_sub: Import your data, structure it with custom field types, save the views each admin needs, and pull instant reports and insights in a few clicks.
module_data.benefits_h2: A database customized the way you need it.
module_data.benefits_sub: Hours, shifts, sequences, tags, custom fields, and the ability to report on any of it in seconds. All in one place, with permissions and segmented views that show each admin exactly what they need to see.
module_data.ben1_title: Bulk import from Excel, CSV or API
module_data.ben1_desc: Import 10,000 profiles per file with field mapping. Batch-assign tags, opportunities, and shifts in the same flow. Or integrate with Vome's API to automate the process.
module_data.ben2_desc: Text, number, date, single & multi-select, email, URL, address, attachment, and more. Unlimited fields per profile.
module_data.ben3_desc: Map volunteer form submissions to database fields, or batch-update data whenever you need to. Merge, archive, or delete profiles to keep your database accurate.
module_data.ben4_desc: Each admin keeps the views that matter to them: new applicants, expiring profiles, top hours, and more. Custom-formula filters for advanced segments.
module_data.ben5_desc: Assign tags, sequences, shifts, languages, or skills. Send broadcast emails or instant messages. All from the profiles you've selected.
module_data.ben6_desc: Attendees, hours, sequence completion, expirations, shift lists, check-ins, emergency contacts, medical info, file uploads, notes, birthdays. Vome has it all, accessible in a few clicks.
module_data.spot1_title: Model the database around the data you need to collect.
module_data.spot1_body: Add unlimited custom fields in every type your program needs: text, number, date, single and multi-select, email, URL, address, attachment, and more. Map application and registration form responses straight into the database so submissions become structured profile data with no re-keying.
module_data.spot1_b1: Map any form question to a database field, so applicants and new registrations populate profiles automatically
module_data.spot1_b2: Manage offline profiles for seniors and participants who don't want to log in, with the same database, hours, and reporting
module_data.spot1_b3: Invite an offline profile to claim their account in one click whenever they're ready to onboard
module_data.spot1_b4: Profile tags for bulk emails, shift assignments, sequence enrollment, and any segmentation your program needs
module_data.spot2_title: Each admin sees exactly the volunteers they're responsible for.
module_data.spot2_body: Each admin can create custom views with the data and filters they need. Permissions then scope what they can see and act on, so it's easy to share resources or segment profiles by site, role, activity, or anything else you need across your team.
module_data.spot2_b1: Custom-formula filters layer multiple conditions for advanced segments, like active volunteers with over 50 hours while also tagged as 'Healthcare'
module_data.spot2_b2: Share a saved view across the team or keep it private to your account
module_data.spot2_b3: Custom admin roles fine-tune edit, delete, message, and report rights per role
module_data.spot2_b4: Export any view to Excel, or sync it with any other database tool for backups and downstream automations
module_data.spot3_title: Pull the answers you need in a few clicks, not a few hours.
module_data.spot3_body: Reports answer the questions your board and funders ask, in seconds. Data refreshes live as your team logs hours, submits forms, and updates profiles. The same filters and date ranges you use in the database carry into every report and dashboard, so the segment you've already built is one click away from a chart.
module_data.spot3_b1: Access custom dashboards and reports that show exactly what you need, and save them for future use
module_data.spot3_b2: Filter every report by site, opportunity, tag, date range, or any field on a profile or shift
module_data.spot3_b3: Track KPIs like retention and activity with ease, all out of the box
module_data.spot3_b4: Export any report to Excel, save its configuration as a custom view to reuse next time, or integrate with Vome's API to pull data anywhere you need it

module_data.midcta_h2: Migrate your existing data in minutes.
module_data.midcta_sub: Customize your fields, drop in an Excel file or connect through the API, and your team is operating from a single source of truth the same day.
module_data.diff_sub: Vome's database software was built to manage volunteers from the ground up: form-mapped applications, onboarding sequences, completed shifts and hours, and communications tools, with permissions and site-level management all included.
module_data.diff1_desc: Profiles include hours, shifts, opportunities, sequence status, tags, and notes by default. No data model setup before you can use it.
module_data.diff2_desc: Scope every admin to their site, location, or team. Visibility, edit rights, and bulk actions all respect those boundaries.
module_data.diff3_desc: Programs migrate from spreadsheets and legacy CRMs in a single working session. Fields customized, profiles imported, views saved, and the team operating that afternoon.
module_data.faq_sub: Common questions about the volunteer database, custom fields, custom views, and reporting.
module_data.faq1_q: Can I import my existing volunteer data?
module_data.faq1_a: Yes. Upload an Excel or CSV file (up to 10,000 profiles per import) and map each column to a Vome field, including any custom fields you've created. The same import flow can batch-assign tags, opportunities, and shifts so volunteers land in the right place from day one.
module_data.faq2_q: What field types can I add to the database?
module_data.faq2_a: Field types include text, number, date, time, single select, multi-select, email, URL, address, attachment, boolean, and unique ID. You can add as many custom fields per profile as your program needs, and map any application or registration form question straight to a field so submissions populate the database automatically.
module_data.faq3_q: How do custom views and permissions work together?
module_data.faq3_a: A custom view is a saved combination of filters, sort order, and visible columns. Each admin can save their own. Permissions per site, location, or team then scope what each admin can see and edit, so a hospital coordinator only ever sees their hospital's volunteers, even when they apply a shared view.
module_data.faq5_a: Attendees, hours, sequence completion, form submissions, and check-in history are available out of the box, each filterable by site, opportunity, tag, date range, or custom field. Analytics dashboards summarize volunteer activity, retention, and program performance for board and funder updates.
module_data.faq6_q: Can I manage volunteers who don't use the app?
module_data.faq6_a: Yes. Create offline profiles for seniors and non-digital participants. Your team manages everything internally: hours, shifts, notes, and reporting all work the same way. Convert an offline profile to an active account anytime by sending an invitation.
module_data.faq7_q: Can I restrict what each admin sees in the database?
module_data.faq7_a: Yes. Scope each admin to one or more sites, locations, or teams, and assign them a custom admin role with a specific permission set. They only see profiles within their scope, the bulk actions and exports respect those boundaries, and shared custom views are filtered automatically when applied. A hospital coordinator never sees clinic volunteers, even when opening a portfolio-wide saved view.
module_data.faq8_q: What actions can I take directly from the database?
module_data.faq8_a: Select any rows in any view and you can: assign tags, sequences, shifts, opportunities, languages, or skills; bulk-update custom field values; send a broadcast email; merge duplicate profiles; archive, reactivate, or delete records. The same actions are available from saved custom views, so a coordinator can act on their segment without leaving the page.
module_data.faq9_a: Built-in dashboards cover Overview, Profiles, Hours, Shifts, Sequences, Forms, and your own Custom Database Fields. The Custom Database Fields dashboard charts any field you've added, with single-select breakdowns, attachment-expiry timelines, and numeric distributions, all without extra configuration. Every dashboard filters by site, opportunity, tag, date range, or any field on the profile.

module_app.page_title: Volunteer mobile app: reserve shifts, check in by QR, message admins | Vome
module_app.page_description: A native iOS and Android app where your volunteers browse opportunities, reserve shifts, bring guests, check in by QR on arrival, manage their schedule and time off, finish onboarding from their phone, and chat with your team in real time.
module_app.hero_h1_part1: Give your volunteers a mobile app that's
module_app.hero_sub: A native iOS and Android app built for volunteers — browse opportunities, reserve shifts, QR check-in on arrival, manage their schedule, sign waivers, watch training videos, and chat with your team. Free for every volunteer, forever.
module_app.hero_meta_multiorg: SSO with Apple, Google or Microsoft
module_app.benefits_h2: Everything your volunteers need, in one app
module_app.benefits_sub: Designed with every age group in mind — from tech-savvy users to those just getting started — the Vome app makes it simple for volunteers to engage with the organizations they care about. Reserving a shift, finishing an onboarding sequence, checking in on arrival, logging hours, getting notified the moment plans change — it all just works.
module_app.ben1_desc: Volunteers explore your available opportunities, filter by role or location, and apply or join with one tap. Application forms render natively, with rich fields and file uploads.
module_app.ben2_desc: Calendar and list views, instant booking or request-to-book, and group reservations so volunteers can bring a friend, family member, or +1 to the same shift.
module_app.ben3_desc: Tap to check in, scan a QR code at the site, or let attendance log automatically — whichever fits your program. Hours roll up to the volunteer's profile, no clipboard or spreadsheet required.
module_app.ben4_desc: Upcoming, past, and time-off views. Cancel a shift, request hours after the fact, or block date ranges so they're not auto-scheduled while they're away.
module_app.ben5_desc: Sign waivers with on-screen e-signature, upload required documents from camera or files, watch training videos, and finish background-check sequences without leaving the app.
module_app.ben6_desc: Direct-message admins, get push reminders before each shift, and join the auto-generated chatroom for every shift volunteers reserve. Voice notes, images, and document attachments included.
module_app.spot1_title: Browse, tap, reserve. With a guest if they want.
module_app.spot1_body: Volunteers see your real-time availability the moment they open the app — calendar grid or list, filtered by role, site, or program. They reserve in one tap, request to book if approval is needed, or kick off a group reservation to bring a friend or family member to the same shift.
module_app.spot1_b1: Calendar and list views with role, location, and cause filters
module_app.spot1_b2: Instant booking or request-to-book per opportunity, set by you
module_app.spot1_b3: Group reservations let one volunteer bring up to N guests, with name and contact for each
module_app.spot1_b4: Self-schedule positions, recurring shifts, and waitlists are all supported in-app
module_app.spot2_title: Tap, scan, or auto-check-in. Hours log themselves.
module_app.spot2_body: Volunteers can pull up a personal QR code for a coordinator to scan at the kiosk, scan a station QR posted at the door, or simply tap to check in from their shift on the home screen. Hours roll up to their profile in real time — no clipboard, no spreadsheet, no manual entry. See every hour-tracking option →
module_app.spot2_b1: Multiple methods to choose from per opportunity: personal QR, station QR, tap-to-check-in, or fully automated
module_app.spot2_b2: Optional geo-fence rejects check-ins from outside the site radius you set
module_app.spot2_b3: Hours auto-roll into the volunteer's profile and the dashboard, no manual entry
module_app.spot2_b4: Volunteers can also claim hours after the fact for shifts they checked in offline
module_app.spot3_title: The whole onboarding sequence including waivers, docs, training — done from the app.
module_app.spot3_body: Drop a volunteer into one of your sequences and they finish every step from their phone. E-sign waivers with their finger, upload documents from camera or file picker, watch training videos, complete background checks, and confirm profile fields — all without ever opening a laptop. Coordinators see progress in real time and get pinged when a step needs review.
module_app.spot3_b1: On-screen e-signature with timestamp + IP capture, stored on the volunteer's profile
module_app.spot3_b2: Document upload from camera, photo library, or file picker (PDFs, images, IDs)
module_app.spot3_b3: Embedded training videos and YouTube playlists, with completion tracking per step
module_app.spot3_b4: Background-check sequences kick off straight from the app and surface back in the dashboard
module_app.midcta_h2: Your volunteers can be browsing shifts before lunch.
module_app.midcta_sub: Send them an invite link, they download the app, sign in with Apple, Google or Microsoft, and they're in. No paywall, no per-volunteer fee, ever.
module_app.diff_h2: More than a mobile site in a shell
module_app.diff_sub: A lot of volunteer apps are wrapped web pages. Vome's app is built native — fast on older phones, real push notifications, real camera, real offline behavior. And it's free for every volunteer, on every plan.
module_app.diff1_title: Free for volunteers, on every plan
module_app.diff1_desc: We never charge volunteers a cent — not for the app, not to apply, not to reserve. The price of the platform is paid by the organization, not the people doing the work.
module_app.diff2_title: One app for every organization they serve
module_app.diff2_desc: A volunteer who serves at a hospital, a food bank, and a school doesn't need three apps. They search for each organization in Vome, join, and switch between them in the side menu.
module_app.diff3_title: Sign in with what they already use
module_app.diff3_desc: Apple, Google, and Microsoft sign-in are wired in alongside email + password. Volunteers don't need to invent a new account or remember another password to start contributing.
module_app.faq_sub: Common questions about the volunteer mobile app.
module_app.faq1_q: Is there a native iOS and Android app, and where do volunteers download it?
module_app.faq1_a: Yes. The Vome volunteer app is in the Apple App Store and Google Play. Volunteers search "Vome volunteer" or open an invite link from your team and the app store opens to the right listing.
module_app.faq2_q: Do volunteers have to pay or create an account before they can sign up?
module_app.faq2_a: Volunteers never pay anything to use Vome. They create a free account once — using Apple, Google, Microsoft, or email — and that one account works across every organization on Vome they choose to join.
module_app.faq3_q: Can a volunteer belong to more than one organization in the same app?
module_app.faq3_a: Yes. The app has a built-in organization search where volunteers can find and join any nonprofit using Vome. Once joined, they switch between their organizations from the side menu and see the right schedule, chats, and onboarding for each one. The same volunteer can serve five different orgs from the same app on the same phone.
module_app.faq4_q: Can volunteers actually finish onboarding from the phone — waivers, ID, training?
module_app.faq4_a: Yes, every step. Volunteers e-sign waivers with their finger, upload documents from the camera or photo library, watch embedded training videos, and complete background-check steps without leaving the app. Each completion writes back to their profile and the dashboard shows coordinators the live status of every sequence.
module_app.faq5_q: What happens at the door — how does check-in actually work?
module_app.faq5_a: There are tons of different check-in methods available. We recommend checking our hour tracking page to learn more about all the options available.
module_app.faq6_q: Can volunteers cancel a shift or block dates they're unavailable?
module_app.faq6_a: Yes. From the schedule tab they can cancel any shift inside the cancellation window you set (admins are notified automatically). They can also add time-off date ranges so the system won't auto-schedule them while they're away — useful for vacations, exam weeks, or surgery recoveries.

module_integrations.page_title: Integrations & API for volunteer management | Vome
module_integrations.page_description: Connect Vome to Salesforce, Sterling Volunteers, SCORM Cloud, Double the Donation, Google Sheets, Zapier, and Microsoft Power Automate. SAML SSO. REST API and custom webhooks for everything else.
module_integrations.hero_sub: Vome offers native integrations with Salesforce, Sterling Volunteers, Microsoft Entra ID, and more. Build no-code automation flows through Zapier and Power Automate. A full REST API when you need to build something custom.
module_integrations.benefits_h2: Connect Vome to your stack without rebuilding it
module_integrations.benefits_sub: Vome talks to the systems you already pay for. Volunteer activity can flow across your background-check tools, CRMs, your inbox, or other custom-built systems.
module_integrations.ben1_desc: Two-way sync between Vome and Salesforce lets you sync any data you need between the platforms, completely out of the box. No CSV exports, no copy-paste.
module_integrations.ben2_desc: Trigger Sterling Volunteers or Verified First checks from an onboarding step. Results land on the volunteer profile and unlock the next step automatically.
module_integrations.ben3_desc: Host SCORM courses through SCORM Cloud, deliver them as a step in any sequence, and keep scores on the volunteer record.
module_integrations.ben4_title: Matching gifts on every hour claim
module_integrations.ben4_desc: Double the Donation runs employer matching when volunteers log hours. Vome surfaces the match in their profile and on reports.
module_integrations.ben5_desc: Connect a Google or Microsoft mailbox so confirmation, reminder, and onboarding emails send from your address, not a Vome subdomain.
module_integrations.ben6_title: SAML SSO for staff and admins
module_integrations.ben6_desc: Provision admins through Microsoft Entra ID, JumpCloud, or any SAML 2.0 provider. Enforce SSO and offboard from one place.
module_integrations.spot1_title: Native integrations for the essentials, hand-built by Vome.
module_integrations.spot1_body: Native integrations with popular providers like Salesforce, Sterling Volunteers, SCORM Cloud, Double the Donation, and Google Sheets make it easy to plug Vome into your tools in a few clicks. Configure them once, and the data flows where you'd expect it: onto the volunteer profile, into your sequences, and across reports.
module_integrations.spot1_b1: Two background-check providers supported: Sterling Volunteers and Verified First, with results stored on the profile
module_integrations.spot1_b2: Map any Vome field to its Salesforce counterpart, including custom fields — for a seamless two-way integration
module_integrations.spot1_b3: SCORM Cloud completion scores arrive in real time and unlock the next onboarding step automatically
module_integrations.spot1_b4: Two-way Google Sheets sync keeps a live mirror of your database for downstream reporting and BI tools
module_integrations.spot2_title: Reach 5,000+ apps without writing a single line of code.
module_integrations.spot2_body: Vome ships with native Zapier and Microsoft Power Automate connectors. Trigger flows on a new applicant, a status change, a new reservation, or a logged hour — then chain the apps your team already lives in.
module_integrations.spot2_b1: Pre-built Vome triggers in both connectors — pick a trigger, pick an action, no API config or schema mapping required
module_integrations.spot2_b2: Reach 5,000+ apps through Zapier and the full Microsoft 365 ecosystem through Power Automate
module_integrations.spot2_b3: Filter and branch flows by site, program, or volunteer attribute so the same trigger forks per audience
module_integrations.spot2_b4: Operations teams own and monitor the flows, no Vome dev access or developer involvement required
module_integrations.spot3_title: A REST API and webhook system for the work that needs custom code.
module_integrations.spot3_body: When the work needs custom code, Vome offers a full REST API and a custom webhook system. Your developers can read and write across the Vome data model, subscribe to the events that matter to your stack, and build the integration your program actually needs. See the developer docs →
module_integrations.spot3_b1: Read and write across the Vome data model — volunteers, opportunities, shifts, hours, and custom fields
module_integrations.spot3_b2: Subscribe webhook endpoints to the events you care about, so your systems react in real time without polling
module_integrations.spot3_b3: A developer dashboard for issuing keys, inspecting payloads, and auditing past deliveries
module_integrations.spot3_b4: Pair the API with Zapier or Power Automate flows so each integration gets the right tool for the job
module_integrations.logos_sub: Grouped by category. Native partners are configured from the Vome admin. Identity providers, email domains, no-code automations, and the developer surface all live alongside them in the same settings area.
module_integrations.diff_sub: Most volunteer platforms either bolt on third-party tools or stop at a single export. Vome ships native, no-code, and developer paths in the same product, so you choose the one that fits.
module_integrations.diff1_desc: Sterling, SCORM, Salesforce, Double the Donation, and Google Sheets are built into Vome. Their data lives on the volunteer profile, not in a separate dashboard.
module_integrations.diff2_desc: Operations teams build flows in Zapier or Power Automate. Developers ship custom integrations through the REST API and webhooks. Same data model under both.
module_integrations.diff3_desc: Through the native Zapier connector alone, Vome can talk to Mailchimp, Blackbaud products, Notion, and thousands more without an engineer. Power Automate covers the Microsoft 365 side. Auth, audit logs, and key rotation come standard.
module_integrations.faq_sub: Common questions about Vome's native integrations, automations, and developer surface.
module_integrations.faq_cta_text: Have a stack we haven't covered?
module_integrations.faq1_q: Which integrations are included with each plan?
module_integrations.faq1_a: Salesforce, SAML SSO, Zapier, Power Automate, and the developer surface (REST API, webhooks, developer dashboard) are available on the Ultimate plan. The full breakdown is in the comparison table above.
module_integrations.faq2_q: How can I integrate Vome with my other tools?
module_integrations.faq2_a: We offer many options to integrate Vome with platforms, with our native integrations or through automated workflows using Zapier or Microsoft Power Automate.
module_integrations.faq3_q: What can I do with the Vome Zapier connector?
module_integrations.faq3_a: Trigger Zaps on new applicants, status changes, hour claims, and form submissions. Chain Vome with Mailchimp, Slack, HubSpot, Outlook, Teams, Google Sheets, or any other Zapier app. The same triggers and actions are available through the Microsoft Power Automate connector for Microsoft 365 shops.
module_integrations.faq4_q: How do the REST API and custom webhooks work?
module_integrations.faq4_a: The developer surface gives your team API keys, a dashboard for monitoring, and the ability to create custom webhook subscriptions per event type. Reach out to our team if you'd like to talk through your specific integration plans.
module_integrations.faq5_q: Which SSO providers does Vome support?
module_integrations.faq5_a: Any SAML 2.0 provider, including Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD), JumpCloud, Okta, and others. Vome supports JIT provisioning of admins through SAML, enforced SSO at the org level, and offboarding when an admin is removed from your IdP.
module_integrations.faq6_q: Can confirmation emails come from our own domain?
module_integrations.faq6_a: Yes. Connect a Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 mailbox to Vome and outbound emails (confirmations, reminders, sequence notifications, automations) send from your address with proper SPF and DKIM, not a Vome subdomain.
module_integrations.faq7_q: Is there a Verified First integration alongside Sterling?
module_integrations.faq7_a: Yes. Vome ships native integrations with both Sterling Volunteers and Verified First, so you can pick the background-check provider you already use. Either way, checks fire from an onboarding step and results land on the volunteer profile.
module_integrations.faq8_a: Yes. Two-factor authentication can be enabled per admin and enforced across your whole team. Pair it with SAML SSO for the strongest posture, or use it on its own.
